actions.name: Create Jira ticket
actions.description: Creates a Jira ticket for newly opened GitHub issues, syncing relevant details such as the issue title and number. It utilizes a GitHub Action to transfer information from GitHub to Jira, ensuring that issues are tracked in the project management system.

actions.name: Sync board list
actions.description: Updates the board JSON in the armbian/armbian.github.io repository based on changes to board configuration files. Sends a repository dispatch event to trigger further processing related to generating a build engine inventory.
actions.execution_method: push to main branch with changes in config/boards/*.*

actions.name: Sync labels
actions.description: Syncs labels from a YAML file to issues and pull requests based on changes in the repository. It utilizes a labeler action to apply labels defined in '.github/labels.yml' when specific conditions are met. The operation can be executed in a dry-run mode for pull requests to preview changes before applying them.
actions.execution_method: workflow_dispatch, push to main branch affecting .github/labels.yml, pull_request affecting .github/labels.yml

actions.name: Sync maintainers
actions.description: Syncs maintainers' information from a contacts database to update the BOARD_MAINTAINER property in board configuration files. If changes are detected, it creates a Pull Request to reflect these updates. Additionally, it identifies inactive maintainers and regenerates the CODEOWNERS file accordingly.

actions.name: Sync tools
actions.description: Updates versions of specified tools in scripts by checking for the latest releases on GitHub and creating a pull request if an update is necessary. Supports multiple tools defined in a matrix, ensuring that the version variable in the corresponding script files is modified accordingly. This acts as a self-created dependabot for tools that cannot be managed by standard dependency tools.
actions.cron_schedule: at 42 minutes past the hour, at 3:00, on day 16

actions.name: Dependency Review
actions.description: Scans dependency manifest files in pull requests to identify known-vulnerable package versions. Blocks merging of pull requests that introduce these vulnerabilities if the workflow run is marked as required. Ensures security by auditing outbound calls during the process.

actions.name: Dispatch to fork
actions.description: Dispatches events to a forked repository based on various GitHub events like push, release, deployment, and pull requests. Utilizes a secret token to trigger a repository dispatch with details about the event, including the reference and SHA. Executes only on forked repositories to facilitate communication with the original repository.

actions.name: Mirror to Codeberg
actions.description: Pushes the contents of the repository to the Codeberg mirror, ensuring that the latest changes are reflected in the mirrored repository. It includes a safety check to prevent forks from executing the action and cancels older runs if a new one starts.

actions.name: Announce merge
actions.description: Announces merges to the main branch by sending a notification to Discord. It retrieves the repository state and formats a message that includes merge details such as the repository name, actor, and commit message.

actions.name: Announce PR
actions.description: Announces pull requests on Discord for review when a specific label is applied. Sends a formatted message containing the pull request details, including the repository and author information. Utilizes a webhook to post the announcement to a designated Discord channel.

actions.name: Auto-label PR
actions.description: Automatically labels pull requests based on size, file categories, and creation date. Removes the 'Ready to merge' label when a PR is updated. Applies labels according to defined thresholds for size and categorization using a configuration file. Additionally, it assigns a quarterly label based on the PR's creation month.

actions.name: Build artifacts
actions.description: Generates artifacts for pull requests labeled with 'Build' by authorized members of the 'Release manager' team. It checks the user's team membership and then compiles the artifacts if the user is authorized. The process includes a concurrency control to manage builds for the same pull request.

actions.name: Check board assets
actions.description: Verifies assets for newly added board configurations by checking for the existence of required images and vendor logos in a specified repository. It identifies newly added board config files in a pull request and validates that corresponding assets exist, providing feedback on any missing items. If assets are missing, it comments on the pull request with instructions for resolution.

actions.name: Analyze kernel security
actions.description: Analyzes Linux kernel security options against hardening standards. Checks kernel configuration files for security issues, excluding unsupported RISC-V configurations, and summarizes findings in the GitHub Actions job summary. Audits outbound calls to enhance security during the analysis process.

actions.name: Clean workflow logs
actions.description: Cleans up workflow logs by deleting runs older than a specified number of days, helping to manage storage and maintain a tidy repository. It can be executed on a schedule or manually with input parameters for customization.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 0:00, on Mon

actions.name: Label PR on approval
actions.description: Labels pull requests as 'Ready to merge' upon approval and removes review-related labels. It downloads the pull request number from a previous workflow run and uses it to manage labels based on approval status. Additionally, it provides feedback through comments on the pull request.

actions.name: Lint scripts (post)
actions.description: Downloads diagnostic artifacts from a previous linting job and posts ShellCheck and shfmt findings as inline review comments on pull requests. It synthesizes a pull request event JSON to facilitate the reviewdog tool's interaction with GitHub's API. This workflow ensures that linting results are communicated effectively, even for pull requests originating from forks.

actions.name: Lint scripts
actions.description: Lints shell scripts in pull requests using ShellCheck, producing a pass/fail check and artifacts for further analysis. It generates findings and auto-fix suggestions that are posted inline on the PR via reviewdog. The workflow ensures that even if linting fails, diagnostic artifacts are still available for review.

actions.name: Listen PR review
actions.description: Saves the pull request number when a review is approved. It uploads the PR number as an artifact for further use. This helps in tracking and managing pull request reviews effectively.

actions.name: Rewrite kernel configs
actions.description: Rewrites kernel configurations and aggregates changes across multiple boards and families. Produces a dynamic matrix of configurations, runs a rewrite script for each entry, collects changes, and opens a pull request summarizing the modifications. The process ensures synchronization of Linux kernel config fragments based on an inventory of boards.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 0:00, on MON

actions.name: Rewrite patches
actions.description: Rewrites kernel or U-Boot patches for a specified board and branch, updating the repository and checking for changes. If changes are detected, it creates a pull request with a summary of the modifications. The process includes cloning the repository, running a compilation script, and generating a detailed report of file changes.

actions.name: Security scan
actions.description: Runs security scans using Scorecards to analyze the repository for vulnerabilities and compliance issues. Publishes the results to the code-scanning dashboard and optionally uploads them as artifacts. The analysis is performed weekly and on pushes to the main branch.
actions.cron_schedule: at 30 minutes past the hour, at 1:00, on Sat

actions.name: Validate board configs
actions.description: Validates changed board configuration files in pull requests by running a Python script that checks for errors and emits annotations on the PR diff. It only processes files that have been modified, ensuring efficient use of CI resources. The workflow blocks merging if any validation errors are detected.

actions.name: Watchdog
actions.description: Monitors and reruns failed jobs for specified workflows, ensuring stability in CI/CD processes. It checks the latest run status and triggers a rerun if the previous attempt failed and hasn't exceeded the maximum retry limit. This helps maintain the reliability of automated workflows.

actions.name: Welcome first-time issue contributor
actions.description: Welcomes first-time issue contributors by sending a personalized message and applying a 'Good first issue' label. It enhances community engagement by encouraging contributors to stay informed and support the project. This process helps create a positive experience for new contributors.

actions.name: Welcome first-time PR contributor
actions.description: Welcomes first-time pull request contributors by sending a personalized message. It acknowledges their contribution and encourages further engagement with the project community. The message includes a link for sharing contact preferences and a reminder to star the repository.
